part_title: Section Fourteen: Season-Long Field IPM Programme
part_subtitle: Season-Long Field IPM Programme for Tuta absoluta

items.text: Chapter Ten
Integrated Management of Tuta absoluta
Introduction

Tuta absoluta is not managed through a single treatment or a fixed spray schedule. It is managed through a sequential programme that begins before transplants reach the field and continues until the final crop residues are disposed of.
items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]Tuta absoluta is not managed through a single treatment or a fixed spray schedule. It is managed through a sequential programme that begins before transplants reach the field and continues until the final crop residues are disposed of.

items.text: Management objectives change during the season. At the beginning, the objectives are to:
items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]Management objectives change during the season. At the beginning, the objectives are to:

items.text: During vegetative growth and fruiting, the objectives shift to:
items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]During vegetative growth and fruiting, the objectives shift to:

items: Prevent larvae from moving into fruits.

items.text: At the end of the season, the objectives are to:
items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]At the end of the season, the objectives are to:

items: Prevent the field from becoming a source for the next crop.
Remove residues and remaining pest stages.
prepared_items: [[prepared]]Prevent the field from becoming a source for the next crop.

items.text: The field programme is not a rigid calendar tied to fixed days after transplanting because the rate of pest development varies with:
items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]The field programme is not a rigid calendar tied to fixed days after transplanting because the rate of pest development varies with:

items.text: The programme is therefore based on plant stage, pest stage, and risk level, rather than chronological age alone.
items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]The programme is therefore based on plant stage, pest stage, and risk level, rather than chronological age alone.

items.text: Governing Principles of the Seasonal Programme
The programme is based on the following principles:
items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]The programme is based on the following principles:

items: 1. Prevention costs less than outbreak control.
2. An infested transplant may establish infestation before monitoring begins.
3. Traps detect male activity but do not replace plant inspection.
4. Eggs and young larvae are the most important intervention stages.
5. Old mines should not be used alone for decision-making.
7. Chemical insecticides are a targeted backup tool, not the foundation of the programme.
8. Field edges and migration sources should be inspected separately.
9. Resistance management begins with the first treatment.
10. The fruiting stage requires a higher protection level than early vegetative growth.
11. Approaching harvest changes the choice of intervention tools.
12. Crop removal is part of pest management, not a separate agricultural operation.
13. Every action should be evaluated by its outcome, not merely by its implementation.
14. All decisions should be documented in the field record.
prepared_items: [[prepared]]1. Prevention costs less than outbreak control.
[[prepared]]2. An infested transplant may establish infestation before monitoring begins.
[[prepared]]3. Traps detect male activity but do not replace plant inspection.
[[prepared]]4. Eggs and young larvae are the most important intervention stages.
[[prepared]]5. Old [[term:mines]]mines[[/term]] should not be used alone for decision-making.
[[prepared]]7. Chemical insecticides are a targeted backup tool, not the foundation of the programme.
[[prepared]]8. Field edges and migration sources should be inspected separately.
[[prepared]]9. Resistance management begins with the first treatment.
[[prepared]]10. The fruiting stage requires a higher protection level than early vegetative growth.
[[prepared]]11. Approaching harvest changes the choice of intervention tools.
[[prepared]]12. Crop removal is part of pest management, not a separate agricultural operation.
[[prepared]]13. Every action should be evaluated by its outcome, not merely by its implementation.
[[prepared]]14. All decisions should be documented in the field record.

items.text: Stage-Based Rather Than Calendar-Based Programming
A conventional calendar programme assumes that an action or spray is performed at fixed intervals.
items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]A conventional calendar programme assumes that an action or spray is performed at fixed intervals.

items.text: A stage-based programme instead depends on:
items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]A stage-based programme instead depends on:

items.text: One week may pass without any need for direct intervention, while another week may require:
items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]One week may pass without any need for direct intervention, while another week may require:

items.text: Choosing not to spray when there is no need is a technical decision, not a deficiency in the programme.
items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]Choosing not to spray when there is no need is a technical decision, not a deficiency in the programme.

items.text: Levels of Prevention and Intervention
The programme consists of five interconnected layers.
items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]The programme consists of five interconnected layers.

items.text: Layer One: Prevent Entry and Establishment
This includes:
items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]This includes:

items.text: Layer Two: Early Detection
This includes:
items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]This includes:

items.text: Layer Three: Biological and Behavioural Suppression
This includes:
items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]This includes:

items.text: Layer Four: Selective Corrective Intervention
This includes:
items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]This includes:

items: Use of a selective insecticide when the intervention level is exceeded.
prepared_items: [[prepared]]Use of a selective insecticide when the intervention level is exceeded.

items.text: Layer Five: Containment and Prevention of Spread
This includes:
items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]This includes:

items.text: The programme should not move directly to layer four before the preceding layers have been assessed.
items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]The programme should not move directly to layer four before the preceding layers have been assessed.

items.text: Preplanting Field-Risk Classification
The field should be classified as low, moderate, or high risk.
items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]The field should be classified as low, moderate, or high risk.

items.text: Low Risk
Moderate Risk

items: Tomato crops are present in the region.
The transplant source is reliable, but regional pest activity is present.
prepared_items: [[prepared]]Tomato crops are present in the region.
[[prepared]]The transplant source is reliable, but regional pest activity is present.

items.text: High Risk

items: An old infested field is adjacent to the site.
Transplants come from an unverified source.
A sorting station or crop residues are nearby.
Severe infestation occurred in the previous season.
Volunteer tomatoes or widespread solanaceous hosts are present.
prepared_items: [[prepared]]An old infested field is adjacent to the site.
[[prepared]]A sorting station or crop residues are nearby.
[[prepared]]Severe infestation occurred in the previous season.
[[prepared]]Volunteer tomatoes or widespread [[term:solanaceae]]solanaceous hosts[[/term]] are present.

items.text: The classification determines:
items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]The classification determines:

items: Need for a preventive biological programme.
Level of coordination with surrounding fields.

items.text: Phase One
Preplanting Planning
Six to Eight Weeks Before Transplanting
Activities include:
items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]Activities include:

items: 3. Reviewing infestation during the previous season.
8. Reviewing insecticides used during the previous season.
prepared_items: [[prepared]]3. Reviewing infestation during the previous season.
[[prepared]]8. Reviewing insecticides used during the previous season.

items.text: A risk list should be prepared before transplants are ordered, not after infestation appears.
items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]A risk list should be prepared before transplants are ordered, not after infestation appears.

items.text: Evaluation of the Previous Crop
The following should be reviewed:
items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]The following should be reviewed:

items: Was the previous crop tomato, potato, or eggplant?
Are pupae likely to remain in the soil?
Was failure recorded for any group?
prepared_items: [[prepared]]Was the previous crop tomato, potato, or eggplant?
[[prepared]]Are [[term:pupation]]pupae[[/term]] likely to remain in the soil?

items.text: Where residues or volunteer plants are present, they should be removed before the new crop is established.
items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]Where residues or volunteer plants are present, they should be removed before the new crop is established.

items.text: Site Selection
Preference should be given to a site that:
items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]Preference should be given to a site that:

items: Is not adjacent to an old, heavily infested tomato field.
Is not close to an open waste pile.
Does not depend on an uninspected nursery.
Allows access to all areas for inspection and spraying.
Has access to climatic data or a nearby weather station.
prepared_items: [[prepared]]Is not adjacent to an old, heavily infested tomato field.
[[prepared]]Is not close to an open waste pile.
[[prepared]]Does not depend on an uninspected nursery.
[[prepared]]Allows access to all areas for inspection and spraying.
[[prepared]]Has access to climatic data or a nearby weather station.

items.text: Distance alone does not guarantee prevention of migration, but it forms part of risk assessment.
items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]Distance alone does not guarantee prevention of migration, but it forms part of risk assessment.

items.text: Review of Crop Rotation
The rotation should be reviewed to confirm that a suitable host is not being maintained continuously.
items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]The rotation should be reviewed to confirm that a suitable host is not being maintained continuously.

items.text: A non-solanaceous crop between tomato crops is preferable, together with:
items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]A non-solanaceous crop between tomato crops is preferable, together with:

items: Removal of potato and eggplant residues where infestation is present.
Prevention of green regrowth after crop termination.
prepared_items: [[prepared]]Removal of potato and eggplant residues where infestation is present.
[[prepared]]Prevention of green regrowth after crop termination.

items.text: Crop rotation does not provide full protection when hosts remain in neighbouring fields.
items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]Crop rotation does not provide full protection when hosts remain in neighbouring fields.

items.text: Cultivar Selection and Crop-Cycle Length
No commercial cultivar is completely immune, but cultivar choice influences the programme through:
items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]No commercial cultivar is completely immune, but cultivar choice influences the programme through:

items: Plant capacity to compensate for damage.

items.text: Long-cycle cultivars require:
items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]Long-cycle cultivars require:

items: More precise management of overlapping generations.
Early planning for the preharvest period.

items.text: Programme Budgeting
Before the season, the costs of the following should be defined:
items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]Before the season, the costs of the following should be defined:

items.text: Advance planning prevents emergency decisions based only on the cheapest available insecticide.
items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]Advance planning prevents emergency decisions based only on the cheapest available insecticide.

items.text: Preparation of the Approved-Materials List
The project should maintain a register showing:
items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]The project should maintain a register showing:

items.text: A material should not be introduced into the programme merely because it is widely used in the region.
items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]A material should not be introduced into the programme merely because it is widely used in the region.

items.text: Preparation of the Resistance-Management Plan
The plan includes:
items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]The plan includes:

items: Groups used during the previous season.
Approximate division of the season into windows.
prepared_items: [[prepared]]Approximate division of the season into windows.

items.text: Final insecticide names should not be selected before the dominant pest stage and crop stage are known.
items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]Final insecticide names should not be selected before the dominant pest stage and crop stage are known.

The following should be prepared:
items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]The following should be prepared:

items.text: Reading procedures should be calibrated among team members before the season begins.
items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]Reading procedures should be calibrated among team members before the season begins.

items.text: Phase Two
Nursery and Transplant Management
Nursery Programme
The Tuta programme begins in the nursery, not after transplanting.
items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]The Tuta programme begins in the nursery, not after transplanting.

items.text: It includes:
items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]It includes:

items: Prevention of unnecessary old tomato transplants remaining in the nursery.
prepared_items: [[prepared]]Prevention of unnecessary old tomato transplants remaining in the nursery.

items.text: The nursery objective is:
items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]The nursery objective is:

items.text: Zero infested transplants at delivery
Not merely reduction of infestation.
items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]Not merely reduction of infestation.

items.text: Inspection of a Transplant Batch
The batch should be inspected before loading and again upon arrival.
items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]The batch should be inspected before loading and again upon arrival.

items.text: Inspection should include:
items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]Inspection should include:

items.text: Batches should be separated according to:
items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]Batches should be separated according to:

items.text: A batch should not be accepted when infestation is distributed or multiple larval instars indicate establishment of a population within the nursery.
items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]A batch should not be accepted when infestation is distributed or multiple larval instars indicate establishment of a population within the nursery.

items.text: Transplant Record
Each batch should be accompanied by a record including:
items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]Each batch should be accompanied by a record including:

items.text: The record should be integrated into the field file.
items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]The record should be integrated into the field file.

items.text: Management of Suspect Transplants
When a suspect plant or tray is detected:
items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]When a suspect plant or tray is detected:

items.text: The batch should not be treated quickly with an insecticide and moved immediately without confirming the absence of eggs and larvae.
items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]The batch should not be treated quickly with an insecticide and moved immediately without confirming the absence of eggs and larvae.

items.text: Phase Three
Field Preparation Before Transplanting
Removal of Infestation Sources
Before transplanting, remove:
items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]Before transplanting, remove:

items.text: The area surrounding the field should be inspected, not only the planting lines.
items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]The area surrounding the field should be inspected, not only the planting lines.

items.text: Trap Installation
Monitoring traps should be installed before or at transplanting to establish a baseline for male activity.
items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]Monitoring traps should be installed before or at transplanting to establish a baseline for male activity.

items.text: The following should be observed:
items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]The following should be observed:

items: Place one or more traps near the main risk direction.
Do not confuse monitoring traps with mass-trapping devices.
Replace lures according to product instructions.
Read traps on a fixed schedule.
prepared_items: [[prepared]]Place one or more traps near the main risk direction.
[[prepared]]Do not confuse monitoring traps with mass-trapping devices.

items.text: Trap numbers alone should not be used to make an intervention decision before plant inspection is conducted.
items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]Trap numbers alone should not be used to make an intervention decision before plant inspection is conducted.

items.text: Dividing the Field into Sectors
The field should be divided according to:
items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]The field should be divided according to:

items.text: Each sector should receive an independent code.
items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]Each sector should receive an independent code.

items.text: This prevents a high-infestation hotspot from being hidden within a low overall field average.
items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]This prevents a high-infestation hotspot from being hidden within a low overall field average.

items.text: Identification of High-Risk Edges
Edges should be identified when they:
items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]Edges should be identified when they:

items: Are near a road or loading area.
Are close to residues or alternative hosts.
Face prevailing winds from an infestation source.
prepared_items: [[prepared]]Are near a road or loading area.
[[prepared]]Are close to residues or alternative hosts.
[[prepared]]Face prevailing winds from an infestation source.

items.text: Edges and the field interior should be inspected as separate units.
items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]Edges and the field interior should be inspected as separate units.

items.text: Phase Four
Transplanting Day and the First Week
Inspection of Transplants During Planting
Workers should be trained to reject:
items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]Workers should be trained to reject:

items.text: Rejected plants should be placed in a container and not left at the field edge.
items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]Rejected plants should be placed in a container and not left at the field edge.

items.text: Recording the Source of Each Sector
When different batches are planted, the map should show:
items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]When different batches are planted, the map should show:

items.text: This helps identify the source of early infestation when it appears in a particular sector.
items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]This helps identify the source of early infestation when it appears in a particular sector.

items.text: Post-Transplant Inspection
During the first days, inspect:
items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]During the first days, inspect:

items.text: Infestation appearing after several days should not automatically be assumed to have originated in the field; it may have been carried with the transplants.
items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]Infestation appearing after several days should not automatically be assumed to have originated in the field; it may have been carried with the transplants.

items.text: Management of an Early Hotspot
When a limited number of plants is infested:
items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]When a limited number of plants is infested:

items: Remove severely infested plants when justified.
Select a biological or selective intervention according to the pest stage.
prepared_items: [[prepared]]Select a biological or selective intervention according to the pest stage.

items.text: A general treatment should not be applied automatically if infestation remains localized.
items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]A general treatment should not be applied automatically if infestation remains localized.

items.text: Phase Five
From the Second Week to the Beginning of Rapid Vegetative Growth
Stage Objectives
The objectives are to:
items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]The objectives are to:

items: Prevent establishment of the first generation.
Prevent movement of infestation among plants.

items.text: This stage is more suitable for preventive biological control than waiting until larval numbers increase.
items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]This stage is more suitable for preventive biological control than waiting until larval numbers increase.

items.text: Inspection Frequency
The field should be inspected at least once weekly under low risk and twice or more when:
items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]The field should be inspected at least once weekly under low risk and twice or more when:

items.text: The inspection interval should not remain fixed when egg development is rapid.
items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]The inspection interval should not remain fixed when egg development is rapid.

items.text: Inspection of Growing Points and Young Leaves
Inspect:
items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]Inspect:

items: The upper parts of the plant.
The two or three newest leaves.

items.text: Record:
items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]Record:

items.text: Differentiate new from old mines using:
items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]Differentiate new from old mines using:

items.text: Initiation of Biological Control
Egg-parasitoid release should begin when fresh eggs are present or when a locally validated preventive programme supports early release.
items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]Egg-parasitoid release should begin when fresh eggs are present or when a locally validated preventive programme supports early release.

items.text: Consider:
items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]Consider:

items: Repeat release according to new egg presence.
prepared_items: [[prepared]]Repeat release according to new egg presence.

items.text: Predators should be introduced early enough to reproduce before the pest population increases.
items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]Predators should be introduced early enough to reproduce before the pest population increases.

items.text: Use of Bt or Viruses
A registered agent should be used when:
items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]A registered agent should be used when:

items: L1 and L2 larvae begin to appear.
prepared_items: [[prepared]]L1 and L2 larvae begin to appear.

items.text: The following should then be reassessed:
items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]The following should then be reassessed:

items.text: Treatment should not be repeated merely because old mine symptoms remain visible.
items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]Treatment should not be repeated merely because old mine symptoms remain visible.

items.text: Phase Six
Active Vegetative Growth
Change in Risk Level
As the canopy expands:
items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]As the canopy expands:

items: Mines become hidden within the plant.
Hotspots may develop beyond the field edges.
prepared_items: [[prepared]]Hotspots may develop beyond the field edges.

items.text: The following should therefore be adjusted:
items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]The following should therefore be adjusted:

items.text: Irrigation and Fertilization Management
The objective is balanced growth rather than excessive vegetative development.
items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]The objective is balanced growth rather than excessive vegetative development.

items.text: Avoid:
items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]Avoid:

items.text: Excessive canopy growth:
items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]Excessive canopy growth:

items: Prolongs the presence of tender tissue.

items.text: Water stress or nutrient deficiency should not be used as a control method.
items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]Water stress or nutrient deficiency should not be used as a control method.

items.text: Sprayer Recalibration
As plant size changes, the sprayer should be recalibrated to verify:
items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]As plant size changes, the sprayer should be recalibrated to verify:

items.text: Water-sensitive paper or another suitable verification method should be used where possible.
items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]Water-sensitive paper or another suitable verification method should be used where possible.

items.text: Decision Not to Intervene
Monitoring may continue without intervention when:
items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]Monitoring may continue without intervention when:

items.text: The reason for non-intervention should be documented so that it is not later interpreted as neglect.
items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]The reason for non-intervention should be documented so that it is not later interpreted as neglect.

items.text: Decision to Intensify Biological Intervention
Biological intervention should be strengthened when:
items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]Biological intervention should be strengthened when:

items: Pest density remains low to moderate.
No toxic pesticide residues are present.
The agent can be distributed effectively.

items.text: Biological releases alone should not be expected to stop a severe outbreak within a few days.
items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]Biological releases alone should not be expected to stop a severe outbreak within a few days.

items.text: Decision on Selective Chemical Intervention
A selective insecticide may be considered when:
items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]A selective insecticide may be considered when:

items: Infestation is moving toward growing points and clusters.
The local intervention threshold is expected to be exceeded.
Other methods cannot act quickly enough.
prepared_items: [[prepared]]Infestation is moving toward growing points and clusters.
[[prepared]]The local intervention threshold is expected to be exceeded.

items.text: The insecticide should be selected according to:
items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]The insecticide should be selected according to:

items.text: Phase Seven
Flowering and Fruit Set
Sensitivity of the Stage
Damage becomes more serious because larvae may move from leaves to:
items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]Damage becomes more serious because larvae may move from leaves to:

items.text: Loss of growing points or leaves close to clusters also affects:
items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]Loss of growing points or leaves close to clusters also affects:

items.text: Tolerance for increasing larval numbers is therefore lower than during early vegetative growth.
items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]Tolerance for increasing larval numbers is therefore lower than during early vegetative growth.

items.text: Cluster Inspection
Inspect:
items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]Inspect:

items.text: Record:
items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]Record:

items.text: Inspection should not be limited to outer leaves.
items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]Inspection should not be limited to outer leaves.

items.text: Conservation of Natural Enemies
At this stage, predator and parasitoid populations should begin to establish if the programme has been managed correctly.
items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]At this stage, predator and parasitoid populations should begin to establish if the programme has been managed correctly.

items.text: The following should be done:
items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]The following should be done:

items: Separate insecticide applications from biological releases.
Monitor plant injury caused by omnivorous mirids.
prepared_items: [[prepared]]Monitor plant injury caused by omnivorous mirids.

items.text: A broad treatment should not be applied merely because a limited number of mines is present when biological suppression is evident.
items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]A broad treatment should not be applied merely because a limited number of mines is present when biological suppression is evident.

items.text: Preventing Larval Movement into Fruits
This is achieved by:
items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]This is achieved by:

items: Maintaining effective residues during hatching when needed.
Not leaving an old neighbouring field unmanaged.
prepared_items: [[prepared]]Maintaining effective residues during hatching when needed.
[[prepared]]Not leaving an old neighbouring field unmanaged.

items.text: Once a larva enters a fruit, the efficacy of many direct interventions declines.
items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]Once a larva enters a fruit, the efficacy of many direct interventions declines.

items.text: Phase Eight
Fruit Growth and Filling
Shift in Focus from Leaves to Fruits
At this stage, percentage leaf mining alone is insufficient for estimating economic damage.
items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]At this stage, percentage leaf mining alone is insufficient for estimating economic damage.

items.text: The following indicators should become central to decision-making:
items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]The following indicators should become central to decision-making:

items: Number of fruits containing live larvae.

items.text: Leaf damage may remain moderate while fruit loss increases.
items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]Leaf damage may remain moderate while fruit loss increases.

items.text: Collection of Infested Fruits
Infested fruits should be collected, particularly when:
items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]Infested fruits should be collected, particularly when:

items: Larvae may move to other fruits.

items.text: Weight loss should be calculated to assess the economic value of the operation.
items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]Weight loss should be calculated to assess the economic value of the operation.

items.text: Fruit Sampling
The sample should be divided into:
items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]The sample should be divided into:

items.text: Record:
items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]Record:

items.text: Fruit infestation by number (%) = Number of infested fruits ÷ Total fruits examined × 100
Weight loss (%) = Weight of fruits rejected because of Tuta ÷ Total sample weight × 100
Both indicators should be used because fruit sizes vary.
items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]Both indicators should be used because fruit sizes vary.

items.text: Intervention Level During Fruiting
The tolerance level is lower when:
items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]The tolerance level is lower when:

items: The market requires a high visual standard.
Infestation beneath the calyx is difficult to detect.
prepared_items: [[prepared]]The market requires a high visual standard.
[[prepared]]Infestation beneath the calyx is difficult to detect.

items.text: Processing tomatoes should be evaluated according to:
items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]Processing tomatoes should be evaluated according to:

items: Effects of infestation on rot and quality.
Cost of harvesting and transporting unacceptable fruits.
prepared_items: [[prepared]]Effects of infestation on rot and quality.
[[prepared]]Cost of harvesting and transporting unacceptable fruits.

items.text: Processing use does not mean accepting fruits containing larvae or rot without limits.
items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]Processing use does not mean accepting fruits containing larvae or rot without limits.

items.text: Canopy Management
Sufficient canopy cover should be maintained to protect fruits from:
items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]Sufficient canopy cover should be maintained to protect fruits from:

items.text: Large numbers of leaves should not be removed merely to eliminate mines if this exposes the fruits.
items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]Large numbers of leaves should not be removed merely to eliminate mines if this exposes the fruits.

items.text: Hotspots should be managed without causing canopy collapse.
items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]Hotspots should be managed without causing canopy collapse.

items.text: Phase Nine
Beginning of Ripening
Preharvest Programme Review
A list of all applications should be prepared, including:
items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]A list of all applications should be prepared, including:

items.text: No treatment should be applied without reviewing this list.
items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]No treatment should be applied without reviewing this list.

items.text: Change in Intervention Tools Near Harvest
As harvest approaches:
items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]As harvest approaches:

items: Collection of infested fruits becomes more important.
Registered Bt and other suitable short-PHI agents become more important.
Timely economic harvest becomes more important.
prepared_items: [[prepared]]Collection of infested fruits becomes more important.
[[prepared]]Registered Bt and other suitable short-[[term:pesticide-safety]]PHI[[/term]] agents become more important.

items.text: A product should not be selected merely because it has a short preharvest interval if it is ineffective against the stage present.
items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]A product should not be selected merely because it has a short preharvest interval if it is ineffective against the stage present.

items.text: Decision to Advance Harvest
Earlier harvest may be considered when:
items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]Earlier harvest may be considered when:

items: Fruit infestation is expected to increase rapidly.
A major migration wave is present.
Fruits meet factory or market requirements.
The cost of waiting exceeds the cost of earlier harvest.
prepared_items: [[prepared]]Fruit infestation is expected to increase rapidly.
[[prepared]]The cost of waiting exceeds the cost of earlier harvest.

items.text: The decision should be made jointly by:
items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]The decision should be made jointly by:

items.text: Early harvest should not substitute for sound management during the season.
items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]Early harvest should not substitute for sound management during the season.

items.text: Residue Analysis
Analysis may be required when:
items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]Analysis may be required when:

items: Supplying a factory with a defined list.
Drift from a neighbouring field is suspected.
prepared_items: [[prepared]]Supplying a factory with a defined list.
[[prepared]]Drift from a neighbouring field is suspected.

items.text: The sample should represent the sector rather than consist of a few selectively chosen fruits.
items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]The sample should represent the sector rather than consist of a few selectively chosen fruits.

items.text: Phase Ten
Harvest
Inspection During Harvest
Workers should be trained to separate:
items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]Workers should be trained to separate:

items.text: A clear route should be assigned for rejected produce.
items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]A clear route should be assigned for rejected produce.

items.text: Rejected fruits should not be discarded between rows or near loading points.
items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]Rejected fruits should not be discarded between rows or near loading points.

items.text: Hand Harvesting
Repeated harvesting allows:
items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]Repeated harvesting allows:

items: Reduced persistence of infested fruits in the field.
prepared_items: [[prepared]]Reduced persistence of infested fruits in the field.

items.text: However, repeated worker entry may:
items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]However, repeated worker entry may:

items.text: Containers should therefore be cleaned and worker movement organized.
items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]Containers should therefore be cleaned and worker movement organized.

items.text: Mechanical Harvesting of Processing Tomatoes
Before mechanical harvest, evaluate:
items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]Before mechanical harvest, evaluate:

items.text: The harvesting machine should be cleaned after heavily infested fields, especially before moving to a young field or another region.
items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]The harvesting machine should be cleaned after heavily infested fields, especially before moving to a young field or another region.

items.text: Continuing Monitoring During Harvest
Monitoring should not stop when picking begins because:
items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]Monitoring should not stop when picking begins because:

items: Some fruits remain in the field.
The field may become a source.
The harvest period may be prolonged.

items.text: Inspection should continue until a clear crop-termination decision is made.
items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]Inspection should continue until a clear crop-termination decision is made.

items.text: Phase Eleven
End of Harvest and Crop Termination
Determining the Economic End Point
The point should be identified at which the value of remaining fruits becomes lower than:
items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]The point should be identified at which the value of remaining fruits becomes lower than:

items: Risk posed by the field to neighbouring crops.
prepared_items: [[prepared]]Risk posed by the field to neighbouring crops.

items.text: A field should not be kept alive for a marginal yield if it is producing large numbers of moths.
items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]A field should not be kept alive for a marginal yield if it is producing large numbers of moths.

items.text: Termination Protocol
This includes:
items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]This includes:

items: 4. Preventing the plant from continuing as a host.
8. Managing the soil according to the production system.
10. Continuing trap monitoring for a period after removal.
prepared_items: [[prepared]]4. Preventing the plant from continuing as a host.
[[prepared]]8. Managing the soil according to the production system.
[[prepared]]10. Continuing trap monitoring for a period after removal.

items.text: Termination is not complete when plants are cut and left on the soil surface.
items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]Termination is not complete when plants are cut and left on the soil surface.

items.text: Assessment of Moth Emergence After Removal
Traps around the field should be used to assess:
items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]Traps around the field should be used to assess:

items.text: Catch may temporarily increase when plants are disturbed, so young neighbouring fields should be warned before crop removal.
items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]Catch may temporarily increase when plants are disturbed, so young neighbouring fields should be warned before crop removal.

items.text: Postharvest Period
During the following weeks:
items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]During the following weeks:

items: Review the efficacy of residue treatment.

items.text: This period is the beginning of the following season’s programme.
items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]This period is the beginning of the following season’s programme.

items.text: Response-Escalation Ladder
A five-level ladder is used.
items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]A five-level ladder is used.

Conditions:
items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]Conditions:

items.text: Action:
items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]Action:

items.text: Level Two: Intensified Monitoring
Conditions:
items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]Conditions:

items: A nearby risk source is present.

items.text: Action:
items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]Action:

items.text: Level Three: Biological or Localized Intervention
Conditions:
items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]Conditions:

items.text: Action:
items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]Action:

items: Apply Bt or another registered agent.

items.text: Level Four: Selective Corrective Intervention
Conditions:
items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]Conditions:

items: Clusters or fruits are at risk.

items.text: Action:
items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]Action:

items: Apply a registered insecticide from an appropriate group.
prepared_items: [[prepared]]Apply a registered insecticide from an appropriate group.

items.text: Level Five: Outbreak Management
Conditions:
items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]Conditions:

items.text: Action:

items.text: Weekly Field-Round Protocol
The round consists of six stages.
items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]The round consists of six stages.

items.text: Stage One: Review the Record
Stage Two: Inspect Field Edges
Stage Three: Inspect the Field Interior
Stage Four: Inspect Fruits
Stage Five: Natural Enemies
Stage Six: Decision
Field-Sample Design
The sample should represent:
items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]The sample should represent:

items.text: All plants should not be sampled from one route.
items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]All plants should not be sampled from one route.

items.text: A zigzag route or predetermined distributed points are preferable.
items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]A zigzag route or predetermined distributed points are preferable.

items.text: The counting unit should be standardized, such as:
items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]The counting unit should be standardized, such as:

items: A defined number of leaves per plant.
prepared_items: [[prepared]]A defined number of leaves per plant.

items.text: Inspection Card
Weekly Decision Meeting
Depending on project size, participants may include:
items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]Depending on project size, participants may include:

items: Nursery or factory representative where needed.

items.text: The meeting should review:
items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]The meeting should review:

items.text: One documented decision should be issued for each sector.
items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]One documented decision should be issued for each sector.

items.text: Integration of the Biological-Control Programme
The programme should be based on:
items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]The programme should be based on:

items: Supplementary food for predators where required.

items.text: Releasing parasitoids or predators is not a separate operation from the rest of management. It affects insecticide selection and timing of leaf removal.
items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]Releasing parasitoids or predators is not a separate operation from the rest of management. It affects insecticide selection and timing of leaf removal.

items.text: Integration of the Microbial-Control Programme
Use:
items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]Use:

items: Bt or a virus near hatching.
Fungi when humidity and temperature are suitable.
Nematodes according to pest location and moisture.
Soil treatments when there is evidence that soil pupation is important.
prepared_items: [[prepared]]Fungi when humidity and temperature are suitable.
[[prepared]]Nematodes according to pest location and moisture.
[[prepared]]Soil treatments when there is evidence that soil pupation is important.

items.text: Consider:
items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]Consider:

items.text: All biological products should not be managed in the same way.
items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]All biological products should not be managed in the same way.

items.text: Integration of the Insecticide Programme
An insecticide should be used when:
items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]An insecticide should be used when:

items: The group differs from the previous window.
The product is compatible with harvest timing.
Its effect on natural enemies is acceptable.
prepared_items: [[prepared]]The group differs from the previous window.
[[prepared]]The product is compatible with harvest timing.
[[prepared]]Its effect on natural enemies is acceptable.

items.text: The reason for selection should be recorded, not only the product name.
items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]The reason for selection should be recorded, not only the product name.

items.text: Managing a Migration Wave
When trap catches rise suddenly near an old field:
items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]When trap catches rise suddenly near an old field:

items: 4. Notify the manager of the old field.
6. Use egg parasitoids or hatching-stage interventions when oviposition is confirmed.
7. Do not conduct a general spray based on trap catch alone.
prepared_items: [[prepared]]4. Notify the manager of the old field.
[[prepared]]6. Use egg parasitoids or hatching-stage interventions when oviposition is confirmed.
[[prepared]]7. Do not conduct a general spray based on trap catch alone.

items.text: The objective is to prevent immigrants from establishing a generation within the field.
items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]The objective is to prevent immigrants from establishing a generation within the field.

items.text: Managing a Heat Wave
High temperature affects:
items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]High temperature affects:

items.text: It requires:
items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]It requires:

items: Applying microbial agents at a suitable time.
Avoiding sprays on stressed plants under phytotoxic conditions.
prepared_items: [[prepared]]Applying microbial agents at a suitable time.
[[prepared]]Avoiding sprays on stressed plants under phytotoxic conditions.

items.text: Low trap catch during extreme heat should not automatically be interpreted as population decline because flight activity may change.
items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]Low trap catch during extreme heat should not automatically be interpreted as population decline because flight activity may change.

items.text: Managing Wind and Dust
Wind and dust cause:
items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]Wind and dust cause:

items.text: The programme should:
items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]The programme should:

items: Postpone spraying when label limits are exceeded.
prepared_items: [[prepared]]Postpone spraying when label limits are exceeded.

items.text: Managing Rainfall or Overhead Irrigation
These may:
items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]These may:

items: Wash off Bt, viruses, and some insecticides.
Increase humidity favourable to some fungi.
prepared_items: [[prepared]]Wash off Bt, viruses, and some insecticides.

items.text: Treatment should not be repeated automatically after rainfall. Review:
items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]Treatment should not be repeated automatically after rainfall. Review:

items.text: Programme for Processing Tomatoes
Processing tomatoes are often characterized by:
items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]Processing tomatoes are often characterized by:

items: One harvest or a limited number of harvests.
Economic sensitivity to the percentage of rejected fruits.
A critical period before mechanical harvest.
prepared_items: [[prepared]]One harvest or a limited number of harvests.
[[prepared]]Economic sensitivity to the percentage of rejected fruits.

items.text: The programme therefore focuses on:
items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]The programme therefore focuses on:

items: Coordinating harvest timing with infestation level.
Terminating the field immediately after delivery.

items.text: Programme for Fresh-Market Tomatoes
Fresh-market tomatoes are characterized by:
items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]Fresh-market tomatoes are characterized by:

items: Rejection of fruits bearing holes or rots.
A longer crop duration in some production systems.
Repeated entry of workers and crates.
prepared_items: [[prepared]]Rejection of fruits bearing holes or rots.
[[prepared]]A longer crop duration in some production systems.

items.text: The programme therefore requires:
items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]The programme therefore requires:

items: Strict review of preharvest intervals between pickings.
Organized movement of workers between infested and uninfested fields.
Conservation of natural enemies throughout the harvest period.
prepared_items: [[prepared]]Strict review of preharvest intervals between pickings.
[[prepared]]Organized movement of workers between infested and uninfested fields.
[[prepared]]Conservation of natural enemies throughout the harvest period.

items.text: Short and Long Production Cycles
Short Production Cycle
This generally includes fewer pest generations but may be exposed to a severe migration wave from a preceding crop.
items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]This generally includes fewer pest generations but may be exposed to a severe migration wave from a preceding crop.

items.text: Long Production Cycle
This requires:
items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]This requires:

items: Timely replacement of lures and dispensers.

items.text: The same chemical programme should not be used for both crop cycles without modification.
items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]The same chemical programme should not be used for both crop cycles without modification.

items.text: Application Under Egyptian Conditions
Egyptian production cycles differ in:
items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]Egyptian production cycles differ in:

items: Overlap among tomato, potato, and eggplant crops.
prepared_items: [[prepared]]Overlap among tomato, potato, and eggplant crops.

items.text: Each region should therefore develop a local calendar based on:
items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]Each region should therefore develop a local calendar based on:

items: Insecticide lists accepted by the target market.
prepared_items: [[prepared]]Insecticide lists accepted by the target market.

items.text: One fixed programme should not be used for the Delta, Nubaria, Upper Egypt, and the North Coast.
items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]One fixed programme should not be used for the Delta, Nubaria, Upper Egypt, and the North Coast.

items.text: Operational Programme for a Small Area
In a small field, the agricultural engineer can implement:
items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]In a small field, the agricultural engineer can implement:

items.text: However, small fields may be severely affected by migration when surrounded by infested crops, so ease of inspection does not necessarily mean ease of control.
items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]However, small fields may be severely affected by migration when surrounded by infested crops, so ease of inspection does not necessarily mean ease of control.

items.text: Operational Programme for a Large Area
The area should be divided into sectors, each with:
items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]The area should be divided into sectors, each with:

items.text: A centralized warning system should be used, while interventions should not be standardized across all sectors when infestation levels differ.
items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]A centralized warning system should be used, while interventions should not be standardized across all sectors when infestation levels differ.

items.text: Weekly Performance Indicators
These include:
items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]These include:

items: Outcome of the most recent intervention.

items.text: Trends over time should be displayed, not only the current value.
items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]Trends over time should be displayed, not only the current value.

items.text: Seasonal Performance Indicators
These include:
items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]These include:

items: Duration for which the field remained after the final harvest.
prepared_items: [[prepared]]Duration for which the field remained after the final harvest.

items.text: Calculating Programme Cost
Direct costs include:
items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]Direct costs include:

items.text: Indirect costs include:
items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]Indirect costs include:

items.text: Two insecticides should not be compared by package price alone, but by the cost of protecting one acceptable tonne of produce.
items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]Two insecticides should not be compared by package price alone, but by the cost of protecting one acceptable tonne of produce.

items.text: Diagnosing Programme Failure
When infestation increases, six areas should be reviewed.
items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]When infestation increases, six areas should be reviewed.

items.text: Prevention
Monitoring
Timing

items: Was the correct life stage targeted?

items.text: Application
Compatibility
Resistance and Migration

items: Was there a migration wave from a neighbouring field?
prepared_items: [[prepared]]Was there a migration wave from a neighbouring field?

items.text: Failure should not be managed by adding more sprays before its cause is identified.
items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]Failure should not be managed by adding more sprays before its cause is identified.

items.text: Weekly Plan Template

rows: Fruit infestation or spread of L2–L3

items.text: Common Errors
Error One: Starting the Programme After Mines Appear
The programme should begin before planting.
items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]The programme should begin before planting.

items.text: Error Two: Using a Fixed Spray Schedule
This ignores pest stage, risk, and natural enemies.
items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]This ignores pest stage, risk, and natural enemies.

items.text: Error Three: Relying on Traps Alone
They do not measure eggs, larvae, or fruits.
items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]They do not measure eggs, larvae, or fruits.

items.text: Error Four: Inspecting Only the Edges
Internal hotspots may be present.
items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]Internal hotspots may be present.

items.text: Error Five: Using the Field Average and Hiding Hotspots
Sectors should be assessed separately.
items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]Sectors should be assessed separately.

items.text: Error Six: Delaying Biological Control
Natural enemies require time to establish.
items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]Natural enemies require time to establish.

items.text: Error Seven: Evaluating Treatment Using Old Mines
New mines and live larvae should be measured.
items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]New mines and live larvae should be measured.

items.text: Error Eight: Failing to Adjust the Sprayer as Plants Grow
Coverage declines.
items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]Coverage declines.

items.text: Error Nine: Removing Too Many Leaves
This exposes fruits and harms the crop.
items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]This exposes fruits and harms the crop.

items.text: Error Ten: Ignoring the Nursery Record
An IRAC group may be repeated or infestation may enter with transplants.
items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]An IRAC group may be repeated or infestation may enter with transplants.

items.text: Error Eleven: Failing to Change the Decision Near Harvest
Priorities and preharvest intervals change.
items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]Priorities and preharvest intervals change.

items.text: Error Twelve: Stopping Monitoring During Harvest
The field may become a source.
items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]The field may become a source.

items.text: Error Thirteen: Leaving the Crop After the Final Picking
The pest continues reproducing.
items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]The pest continues reproducing.

items.text: Error Fourteen: Treating Every Failure with an Additional Spray
The cause should first be diagnosed.
items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]The cause should first be diagnosed.

items.text: Error Fifteen: Evaluating the Programme Only by the Number of Sprays
The criteria are sound fruit, cost, and sustainability.
items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]The criteria are sound fruit, cost, and sustainability.

items.text: Practical Priorities in Egypt

items: 3. Separate edges from the interior in monitoring.
4. Inspect eggs and new mines at least weekly.
5. Increase inspection during heat or migration.
6. Begin egg-parasitoid and microbial interventions early.
10. Link every treatment to harvest timing and target market.
11. Measure fruit infestation by number and weight.
12. Avoid extensive removal of canopy in processing tomatoes.
13. Notify young fields before removing an old crop.
15. Terminate the field as soon as it becomes uneconomic.
16. Continue trap monitoring after crop removal.
19. Develop a separate programme for each region and production cycle.
20. Review the season before the next crop begins.

[[prepared]]5. Increase inspection during heat or migration.
[[prepared]]6. Begin egg-parasitoid and microbial interventions early.
[[prepared]]10. Link every treatment to harvest timing and target market.
[[prepared]]11. Measure fruit infestation by number and weight.
[[prepared]]12. Avoid extensive removal of canopy in processing tomatoes.
[[prepared]]13. Notify young fields before removing an old crop.
[[prepared]]15. Terminate the field as soon as it becomes uneconomic.
[[prepared]]16. Continue trap monitoring after crop removal.
[[prepared]]19. Develop a separate programme for each region and production cycle.
[[prepared]]20. Review the season before the next crop begins.

items.text: Scientific Summary of Section Fourteen
A season-long integrated programme for Tuta absoluta is not a spray schedule. It is a sequence of decisions beginning before planting and continuing after harvest.
items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]A season-long integrated programme for Tuta absoluta is not a spray schedule. It is a sequence of decisions beginning before planting and continuing after harvest.

items.text: The programme depends on:
items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]The programme depends on:

items: Division of the field into sectors.
Inspection of eggs and active mines.
Protection of fruits during set and filling.
Insecticide management according to mode of action.
prepared_items: [[prepared]]Protection of fruits during set and filling.
[[prepared]]Insecticide management according to mode of action.

items.text: Monitoring intensity and intervention change with plant stage, temperature, and migration sources.
items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]Monitoring intensity and intervention change with plant stage, temperature, and migration sources.

items.text: Intervention decisions are not made from traps alone but by integrating:
items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]Intervention decisions are not made from traps alone but by integrating:

items.text: The principal turning point in the programme is preventing the pest from progressing from eggs and young larvae to advanced instars and fruits.
items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]The principal turning point in the programme is preventing the pest from progressing from eggs and young larvae to advanced instars and fruits.

items.text: Processing tomatoes require protection of canopy cover, measurement of fruit weight loss, and coordination of harvest timing and field termination.
items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]Processing tomatoes require protection of canopy cover, measurement of fruit weight loss, and coordination of harvest timing and field termination.

items.text: Fresh-market tomatoes require more frequent inspection of fruits and containers and strict review of preharvest intervals during repeated pickings.
items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]Fresh-market tomatoes require more frequent inspection of fruits and containers and strict review of preharvest intervals during repeated pickings.

items.text: The programme is successful when it achieves:
items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]The programme is successful when it achieves:

items: Prevention of pest movement into the following crop.
prepared_items: [[prepared]]Prevention of pest movement into the following crop.
